title: Irish Smoked BBQ Ribs
description: A sweet and savory twist on traditional BBQ ribs, with a smoky depth from Irish whiskey. Perfect for a summer evening.
instructions: 2. In a small bowl, mix together brown sugar, treacle, smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
3. Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs and brush the Irish whiskey on both sides of the ribs.
4. Apply the sugar mixture evenly to both sides of the ribs.
5. Place the ribs on the grill, bone-side down, and close the lid.
6. Cook for 2 hours, or until the ribs reach an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
7. After 2 hours, brush the grill seasoning on both sides of the ribs.
8. Continue cooking for another 30 minutes, or until the ribs reach an internal temperature of 180°F (82°C).
9. Remove the ribs from the grill and let rest for 10 minutes.
10. Cut the ribs into individual portions and serve.
